conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 17 and lives at home with me. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

To assess this properly, I need to ask you some questions about Alex's current situation.

Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's doing his A-levels at the local sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. A-levels would typically count as non-advanced education.

Can you tell me when Alex's birthday is, or specifically, has he already turned 17 or will he be turning 17 soon? I need to confirm his exact age.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's already 17 - turned 17 a few months back.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. 

Since Alex is 17 and in full-time education doing A-levels, I have a few more questions to ensure I can give you an accurate assessment.

Are you currently responsible for Alex? By this, I mean - does he live with you and are you the person who is primarily looking after him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. He lives here with me and I'm the one looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Are you currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

One final question: Is Alex's education or training expected to continue beyond his 20th birthday?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he should be finished well before then. He's just doing his A-levels, which he'll complete when he's 18.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 17 years old, which falls within the age range where Child Benefit can be claimed for children in education or training (16-19 years old)
- He is in full-time non-advanced education (A-levels at sixth form college), which qualifies under the rules
- He lives with you and you are responsible for him
- You live in the UK
- His education will be completed before he turns 20

You should be able to make a claim for Child Benefit for Alex. If you haven't already claimed, you can apply online or by post. Remember that Child Benefit is paid at a weekly rate, and if you or your partner earns over £50,000 per year, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge through your tax return, though you can still claim the benefit itself.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is in full-time non-advanced education (A-levels at sixth form college). He lives with the claimant who is responsible for him. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester). Alex's education will be completed when he is 18, which is before he turns 20. Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit for a child aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training. Since Alex meets all these criteria - he is 17, in full-time non-advanced education (A-levels), lives with and is cared for by the claimant, and his education will finish before age 20 - the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (aged 17), who lives with them in the UK. Alex is currently studying A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as full-time non-advanced education, and will complete this before turning 20. The claimant meets all the requirements for Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.

Since Alex meets all these criteria - he is 17, in full-time non-advanced education (A-levels), lives with and is cared for by the claimant, and his education will finish before age 20 - the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Alex is currently studying A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as full-time non-advanced education, and will complete this before turning 20. The claimant meets all the requirements for Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.
